steps.title: Identify the structural requirement
steps.content: The main subject “Several advantages” (with its appositive explanation set off by em dashes) must be followed by a verb to create a complete clause. The blank must contain that verb, connecting the subject to “acoustic waves as a promising alternative...”

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content: An independent clause requires a finite verb. “Have positioned” is a finite verb phrase that creates the complete main clause: “Several advantages have positioned acoustic waves as a promising alternative...” Choices B, C, and D use non-finite forms—participles (positioning, having positioned) and a prepositional phrase (by positioning)—which cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause. That matches choice A — A is correct.
